evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-advise in full scan mode on a codebase where several skills lack trigger-phrase coverage in their eval cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill loads config and manifest, scans all targets, identifies skills with uncovered trigger phrases, produces a structured adviser_report with a dimensions section containing all five dimensions with per-target severity scoring, and returns needs_user_input with drill-down options listing each gapped dimension. The skill does not call askQuestion and does not modify any files.
assertions: The skill identified at least one skill without full trigger-phrase coverage
The adviser_report includes a dimensions section listing all five dimensions: trigger_phrases, schema_validation, regression_baselines, citation_verification, checklist_completeness
Each dimension in the report has a severity field set to ok, warn, or critical
The skill returned needs_user_input with a drill-down question offering dimension-specific options
The skill did not call askQuestion
The skill did not modify any files

evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-advise in targeted mode scoped to the skill zoto-help-evals.
evals.expected_output: The skill loads config and manifest, filters targets to zoto-help-evals only, assesses that skill across all five dimensions (marking inapplicable dimensions as exempt), and produces per-dimension findings with severity scoring and a score between 0 and 100. Returns needs_user_input with drill-down options for any gapped dimensions.
assertions: The report scope shows mode: targeted with a target_glob matching the specified skill
All five dimensions are present in the report, with inapplicable ones marked exempt
Each non-exempt dimension has a numeric score between 0 and 100
Findings reference the specific target_id for zoto-help-evals
The skill returned needs_user_input after presenting the scan summary
The skill did not call askQuestion

evals.expected_output: The skill detects the missing configuration file and returns needs_user_input pointing the user to /z-eval-configure. It does not crash, does not produce a partial adviser_report, and does not modify any files.
assertions: The skill returned needs_user_input with reason indicating no eval-system config was found
The needs_user_input questions include an option referencing /z-eval-configure
No adviser_report was produced (the skill aborted before scanning)
The skill did not crash or produce an error traceback
The skill did not modify any files
The skill did not call askQuestion

evals.prompt: After /z-eval-advise has completed its scan and the user selects drill-down on all dimensions, verify the recommendation handoff structure.
evals.expected_output: The skill produces detailed per-target findings for each gapped dimension and generates deterministic recommendations. Each recommendation references a specific target_id from the manifest, maps to exactly one handoff command (/z-eval-create for targets without eval files, /z-eval-update for targets with existing but incomplete evals), and includes actionable args (target path or glob, mode flags). Returns needs_user_input at the second breakpoint offering action choices.
assertions: Each recommendation references a specific target_id from the manifest
Each recommendation maps to exactly one handoff command: either /z-eval-create or /z-eval-update
Recommendations for targets without eval files use /z-eval-create
Recommendations for targets with existing evals use /z-eval-update with --target and --apply args
The second needs_user_input breakpoint offers action options including accept-all, walk, create-only, update-only, and none
The skill did not call askQuestion
The skill did not modify any files
